prediction: THAT day will be a day of great distress, of anguish and terror, of darkness and gloominess. However, “those calling on Jehovah for help will be saved.” God will destroy his enemies and save his people. For this reason, the prophet Zephaniah declared: “The day of Jehovah is near—near, and it is hastening greatly!” When will this divine judgment come?

Jehovah’s “great and fear-inspiring day” is certain to come upon this wicked system of things, even as the Flood drowned the wicked world in Noah’s day. There is no escaping it. Yet, “everyone who calls on the name of Jehovah will get away safe.” God will destroy his enemies and save his people. With a sense of urgency, the prophet Zephaniah declares: “The great day of Jehovah is near. It is near, and there is a hurrying of it very much.” When, though, will this divine judgment be executed?

I aso mua loa, e isi se tamaliki fafine e igoa ki a Lily. Ne fiafia mālosi tou fafine o tafao i tua kae onoono ki mea i ana tafa. I te aso e tasi, ne maua ne ia se fatu matilotilo i te laukele. Ne puke aka ne ia kae fakaasi atu ki tenā mātua.

"Mami, kiloke! Se a te mea tenei?” ne fesili atu a Lily.

“Ko te fatu, taku pele. E mafai o fakaaoga ne koe ke fakagaligali ki ei tau gatu,” ko te tali a tenā mātua.

Ne fiafia mālosi a Lily ki te fatu kae ne fakaaoga ne ia ke fakagaligali ki ei tenā gatu. Ne fakamatamata tou fafine i tenā gatu kae ne fakaasi atu ne ia ki ana taugasoa. Ne fai atu lātou me ko oko eiloa i
reference:
Ne manako mālosi a Lily ke olo lāua mo tenā mātua ki te malae tafaofao. Ne manako a ia o tafao i mea ta‵fao i ei, kae fāgai a mamo. Ne tele a ia ki tenā potu kae puke mai tenā taga. Ne tuku ne ia ki loto i ei a nāi falaoa mō mamo, se fagu vai mo se pulou.

Ne tele atu a ia ki te potu ‵nofo kae fakaasi atu tenā taga ki tenā mātua. “Kiloke, mami, au ko toka!” ne fai atu tou fafine. Ne fakamisikata tenā mātua kae fai atu, “Ko oko eiloa i te gali, e Lily, kae ko puli i a koe se mea.” Ne kilo atu a Lily ki tenā taga mo te mafaufau me se a te mea ko puli i a ia. “Se a te mea ne puli i a au, e mami?” ne fesili atu tou fafine.

“Ko puli i a koe o faka‵mau tou taga, e Lily. Kāfai e se faka‵mau ne koe tou taga, e mafai eiloa o tō ou mea,” ne fai atu tenā mātua. Ne fakaasi atu ne tou fafine ki a Lily te auala ke faka‵mau tenā taga. Ne taumafai a Lily o faka‵mau tenā taga, kae ne faigatā ‵ki. Ne ‵futi kae ‵tuki ne ia te ‵fagu, kae ne seki gasuesue. Ne kaitaua tou fafine kae fai atu, “E se mafai ne au, e mami. Ko tō faigata.”

Ne ‵sai atu tenā mātua ki a ia kae fai atu, “E ‵lei, e Lily. A te faka‵mau o se taga se mea fou ki a koe. E manakogina fua ke fakaakoako koe ki ei. Tenei, ke fesoasoani atu au ki a koe.” Ne puke ne tou fafine te lima o Lily kae takitaki atu ke faka‵mau tenā taga. Ne fakamatamata kae fiafia a Lily. Ne fai atu tou fafine, “Ko oti ne faka‵mau ne au, e mami. Ko oti ne faka‵mau taku taga. Fakafetai mo te fesoasoani mai.”

Ne fai atu tenā mātua, “E se a, e Lily. Ko oko eiloa i te gali tau galuega. Ne taumafai mālosi koe kae ne tauloto ne koe se mea fou. Nei la, ko toka koe ke olo ki te malae tafao?” Ne lūlū tou fafine kae fai atu, “Ao, e mami. Au ko toka. Ke olo la!” Ne puke ne ia tenā taga kae tele atu ki te mataloa. Ne tautali atu tenā mātua i a ia kae ne olo atu lāua ki te malae tafao. Ne fiafia mālosi a Lily i te taimi ne tafao kae fāgai ei ne ia a mamo. Ne fiafia tou fafine me ne taumafai a ia o faka‵mau tenā taga.
